prompt:
Passage 1
A regional maintenance company recently adopted a digital scheduling system for service calls. Previously, office staff created daily schedules on paper and called technicians when assignments changed. This process worked during slow periods, but it became difficult to manage when several urgent requests arrived at the same time.
The new system allows dispatchers to assign service calls through a shared online schedule. Technicians can view their assignments on mobile devices and receive updates when changes are made. Managers report that technicians now receive information more quickly and that customers are given more accurate arrival windows.
The company also uses the scheduling data to identify busy service periods and plan staffing more effectively. Although the system required training, most employees adjusted after several weeks of regular use.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company stopped accepting urgent service calls.
choices.B: A digital scheduling system improved service-call management.
choices.C: Technicians now create all customer invoices.
choices.D: Paper schedules are more accurate than digital schedules.

explanation: The passage focuses on how the digital scheduling system helped the company manage service calls, update technicians, and give customers more accurate arrival windows.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning February 12, all employees who operate company vehicles must complete the updated fuel-card procedure. The new procedure requires employees to enter the vehicle number and current mileage at the time of each fuel purchase.
The change is intended to improve fuel tracking and help identify unusual usage patterns. Under the previous process, some receipts did not include enough information to match fuel purchases with specific vehicles.
Employees should contact Fleet Services if they have questions about using the fuel card system.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce an updated fuel-card procedure
choices.B: To introduce a new vehicle repair shop

explanation: The notice announces the updated fuel-card procedure for employees who operate company vehicles.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many businesses use customer feedback surveys to understand how well their services are meeting customer needs. Surveys may ask about wait times, employee communication, product quality, billing, or overall satisfaction.
A well-designed survey can help a company identify strengths and weaknesses. For example, if many customers report that service representatives are helpful but response times are slow, managers may decide to adjust staffing or improve scheduling.
However, surveys are most useful when the results are reviewed carefully and connected to specific actions. Collecting feedback without making improvements may reduce customer confidence over time.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Customer surveys can help businesses improve when results are used carefully.
choices.B: Surveys are useful only when customers complain.
choices.C: Companies should avoid asking customers for feedback.

explanation: The passage explains that customer surveys can identify strengths and weaknesses, but their value depends on using the results to make improvements.

prompt:
Passage 4
The development of elevator technology changed the way buildings were designed and used. Before reliable elevators became common, most buildings were limited in practical height because people had to climb stairs to reach upper floors. This made higher floors less desirable for offices or apartments.
As elevators became safer and more dependable, taller buildings became more practical. Businesses could use upper floors more effectively, and cities were able to expand upward rather than only outward. Elevator improvements also influenced building design, construction planning, and daily movement within large structures.
Today, elevators remain essential in many workplaces, hospitals, residential buildings, and public facilities. Regular inspection and maintenance help ensure they continue operating safely.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Elevator technology made taller buildings more practical.
choices.B: Stairs are no longer found in modern buildings.
choices.C: Cities stopped expanding after elevators were invented.
choices.D: Elevators are used only in residential buildings.

explanation: The passage explains how reliable elevators made taller buildings more practical and changed building design and city growth.

prompt:
Passage 5
A manufacturing facility recently began using color-coded floor markings to improve organization and safety. Different colors are used to identify walkways, equipment zones, storage areas, and emergency access routes.
Before the markings were added, employees sometimes placed materials in areas that blocked movement or made equipment harder to reach. Supervisors also noticed that visitors and new employees occasionally had trouble identifying safe walking paths.
The new markings provide visual guidance throughout the facility. Managers report that work areas are easier to navigate and that employees are more consistent about keeping materials in designated locations.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Color-coded floor markings improved organization and safety in the facility.
choices.B: The facility removed all storage areas.
choices.C: Employees no longer need safety procedures.
choices.D: Visitors are not allowed inside the facility.

explanation: The passage focuses on how color-coded floor markings helped improve organization, navigation, and safety.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many organizations use written standard operating procedures, often called SOPs, to describe how routine tasks should be performed. An SOP may explain the steps for starting equipment, handling materials, completing inspections, or documenting work.
SOPs help create consistency because employees can follow the same process each time a task is completed. They are especially useful when training new employees or when tasks must be performed carefully to meet safety or quality requirements.
However, SOPs must be kept up to date. If procedures change but written instructions are not revised, employees may follow outdated steps. For this reason, supervisors often review SOPs after equipment changes, process updates, or safety concerns.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: SOPs help standardize work but must be kept current.
choices.B: Written procedures are useful only for office workers.
choices.D: SOPs are never used during training.

explanation: The passage explains that SOPs help make work consistent, but they must be updated when procedures change.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many towns operate public works departments that maintain streets, sidewalks, drainage systems, signs, and other local infrastructure. Although residents may notice public works crews most often after storms or during road repairs, much of the department’s work occurs throughout the year.
Routine maintenance can prevent small problems from becoming larger and more expensive. For example, clearing drainage areas before heavy rainfall may reduce flooding, while repairing small pavement cracks may help prevent more serious road damage.
Public works departments must often prioritize tasks because budgets, equipment, and staff time are limited. Decisions may be based on safety concerns, urgency, cost, and the number of residents affected.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: The role and priorities of public works departments
choices.C: Why residents should repair public roads themselves
choices.D: How private businesses set product prices

explanation: The passage explains what public works departments do and how they decide which tasks to prioritize.

prompt:
Passage 8
A regional training program recently reviewed completion rates for its apprenticeship preparation course. The course is designed to help applicants improve basic math, reading, and workplace study skills before taking entrance exams.
Program staff found that students who attended at least 80 percent of class sessions were more likely to complete the course than those who attended less often. Students who used the optional practice materials also showed stronger progress on weekly quizzes.
Based on the review, staff decided to remind students about attendance expectations during orientation and provide clearer instructions for using the practice materials. The goal is to help more students stay on track and finish the program.

What was the purpose of the course described in the passage?

choices.A: To help applicants improve basic skills before entrance exams

explanation: The course is designed to help applicants improve basic math, reading, and workplace study skills before entrance exams.
